--   /health</tt>. Use this to disable it entirely (e.g., if you need
--   custom health logic).
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withTransport WebTransport.server
--     |&gt; Application.withoutHealthCheck
--     |&gt; Application.withService myService
--   </pre>
withoutHealthCheck :: Application -> Application

-- | Configure the integration dispatcher.
--   
--   The dispatcher routes events to per-entity workers for outbound
--   integrations. By default, <a>defaultConfig</a> is used which sets a
--   30-second event processing timeout.
--   
--   Use this to customize timeouts, channel capacity, and worker lifecycle
--   for your application's needs.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withDispatcherConfig
--          Dispatcher.defaultConfig { eventProcessingTimeoutMs = Just 120000 }
--     |&gt; Application.withService myService
--   </pre>
withDispatcherConfig :: Typeable config => (config -> DispatcherConfig) -> Application -> Application

-- | Wire a persistent query object store backend into the application.
--   
--   Equivalent to <a>withQueryObjectStore</a>; provided for
--   discoverability in the builder chain alongside <a>withQuery</a> and
--   <a>withService</a>.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withEventStore postgresEventStoreConfig
--     |&gt; Application.useQueryObjectStore (PostgresQueryObjectStoreConfig { ... })
--     |&gt; Application.withQuery @UserOrders
--     |&gt; Application.run
--   </pre>
useQueryObjectStore :: QueryObjectStoreConfig config => config -> Application -> Application

-- | Enable the /ready HTTP endpoint (on by default, shown for
--   discoverability).
--   
--   The /ready endpoint returns 200 when all registered queries have
--   completed their rebuild, and 503 while any query is still replaying.
--   Calling this function is optional — the endpoint is on by default. It
--   is exposed in the public API so Jess can see the feature and
--   understand the <i>health vs </i>ready distinction without reading
--   prose.
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   app = Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.useReadinessEndpoint
--     |&gt; Application.withQuery @UserOrders
--     |&gt; Application.run
--   </pre>
useReadinessEndpoint :: Application -> Application

-- | Health check configuration for WebTransport. When enabled, GET
--   requests to the health path return 200 OK with a JSON body. Enabled by
--   default with path "health". Can be disabled or customized.
data HealthCheckConfig
HealthCheckConfig :: Text -> HealthCheckConfig

-- | URL path segment for the health endpoint (default: "health"). The
--   endpoint will be available at /{healthPath}.
[healthPath] :: HealthCheckConfig -> Text

-- | Configuration for the dispatcher.
data DispatcherConfig
DispatcherConfig :: Int -> Int -> Bool -> Int -> Int -> Maybe Int -> Int -> Int -> DispatcherConfig

-- | How long a worker can be idle before being reaped (milliseconds).
--   Default: 60000 (60 seconds)
[idleTimeoutMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | How often the reaper checks for idle workers (milliseconds). Default:
--   10000 (10 seconds)
[reaperIntervalMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Whether to enable the reaper. Set to False for testing. Default: True
[enableReaper] :: DispatcherConfig -> Bool

-- | Capacity of worker channels (bounded). Events queue up to this limit
--   before backpressure is applied. Default: 100 Sizing rule of thumb:
--   capacity = processing_rate * acceptable_latency
[workerChannelCapacity] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Timeout in milliseconds for writing to a full channel. If exceeded,
--   event is logged as dropped. Default: 5000 (5 seconds)
[channelWriteTimeoutMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Timeout in milliseconds for processing a single event. If exceeded,
--   the event processing is cancelled and the worker continues with the
--   next event. Default: Nothing (no timeout). This prevents slow
--   integrations from blocking a worker indefinitely.
[eventProcessingTimeoutMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Maybe Int

-- | Maximum number of retry attempts for transient IO failures in outbound
--   event processing. Only IO exceptions (e.g., network errors) are
--   retried; application-level errors (e.g., JSON decode failures) are
--   not. Default: 3 (total attempts = initial + 3 retries)
[maxEventRetries] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Base delay in milliseconds for exponential backoff between retries.
--   Actual delays: baseDelay * 2^attempt (e.g., 500ms, 1000ms, 2000ms).
--   Default: 500
[retryBaseDelayMs] :: DispatcherConfig -> Int

-- | Default dispatcher configuration.
defaultConfig :: DispatcherConfig

-- | Internal configuration for file upload routes (after initialization).
--   
--   This is created by <tt>initializeFileUpload</tt> from a
--   <tt>FileUploadConfig</tt>. Users should not create this directly - use
--   <tt>FileUploadConfig</tt> instead.
data FileUploadSetup
FileUploadSetup :: InternalFileUploadConfig -> BlobStore -> FileStateStore -> FileUploadSetup

-- | Upload limits and allowed content types
[config] :: FileUploadSetup -> InternalFileUploadConfig

-- | Where to store file content
[blobStore] :: FileUploadSetup -> BlobStore

-- | Where to track file lifecycle state
[stateStore] :: FileUploadSetup -> FileStateStore

-- | Check if the Application is empty (no configurations set).
isEmpty :: Application -> Bool

-- | Check if a config has been registered.
hasConfig :: Application -> Bool

-- | Check if a QueryRegistry has been configured.
hasQueryRegistry :: Application -> Bool

-- | Check if any ServiceRunners have been configured.
hasServiceRunners :: Application -> Bool

-- | Get the number of ServiceRunners configured.
serviceRunnerCount :: Application -> Int

-- | Check if any transports have been configured.
hasTransports :: Application -> Bool

-- | Get the number of transports configured.
transportCount :: Application -> Int

-- | Check if any query endpoints have been configured.
hasQueryEndpoints :: Application -> Bool

-- | Get the number of query endpoints configured.
queryEndpointCount :: Application -> Int

-- | Check if any query definitions have been registered.
hasQueryDefinitions :: Application -> Bool

-- | Get the number of query definitions registered.
queryDefinitionCount :: Application -> Int

-- | Check if an EventStore has been configured.
hasEventStore :: Application -> Bool

-- | Check if file upload has been configured.
hasFileUpload :: Application -> Bool

-- | Check if authentication has been configured.
hasAuth :: Application -> Bool

-- | Run the application using the configured EventStore.
--   
--   This is the primary way to run an Application. It: 1. Creates the
--   EventStore from the configured settings 2. Wires all query definitions
--   3. Rebuilds queries from historical events 4. Starts live subscription
--   for new events 5. Runs all services
--   
--   Example:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Application.new
--     |&gt; Application.withEventStore postgresConfig
--     |&gt; Application.withTransport WebTransport.server
--     |&gt; Application.withService myService
--     |&gt; Application.withQuery @CartSummary
--     |&gt; Application.run
--   </pre>
run :: Application -> Task Text Unit

-- | Run application with a provided EventStore.
--   
--   Use this when you need to provide your own EventStore instance. For
--   most cases, prefer using <a>run</a> with <a>withEventStore</a>.
--   
--   NOTE: This function only supports file uploads configured with
--   <tt>withFileUpload @()</tt> (i.e., config type is <tt>()</tt>). If
--   your FileUpload factory uses app config, use <a>run</a> instead, which
--   loads config before resolving factories.
--   
--   This function: 1. Wires all query definitions (creates stores,
--   updaters, endpoints) 2. Creates a query subscriber with combined
--   registries 3. Rebuilds all queries from historical events 4. Starts
--   live subscription for new events 5. Collects all command endpoints
--   from all services 6. Runs each transport once with combined endpoints
runWith :: EventStore Value -> Application -> Task Text Unit

-- | Run application with provided EventStore, non-blocking.
--   
--   Launches the application in a background task and returns immediately.
--   Errors are logged to the console rather than silently discarded.
runWithAsync :: EventStore Value -> Application -> Task Text Unit
